steps.title: Use the no-solution shortcut
steps.content: A linear system has no solution when the two lines are parallel and distinct. Rewrite the given equation: $$40x=2000y-6000 \Rightarrow y=\frac{x}{50}+3$$ So the second line needs slope $\frac{1}{50}$ but a different $y$-intercept.

steps.title: Test the matching slope
steps.content: The matching choice, $\frac{1}{25}x=2y$, becomes $y=\frac{x}{50}$. This has the same slope as the given line but intercept $0$ instead of $3$, so the two lines are parallel and never intersect.

steps.title: Eliminate the distractors
steps.content: A choice with slope $\frac{1}{2000}$, so it intersects the given line. A choice with slope $\frac{1}{2}$, so it also intersects. A choice with the same as the given line, which would give infinitely many solutions. Only the matching choice gives parallel distinct lines.
